markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ latest delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, for additional polish has reset the timeline after an earlier shift from a 2025 target to May 2026, leaving roughly five months until launch and heightening anticipation for major cultural milestones in the interim. The open-world title’s return to Vice City continues to dominate gaming discourse, with Rockstar and parent Take-Two Interactive historically prioritizing quality over speed, as evidenced by past delays that allowed the franchise to set new sales benchmarks. Traders are monitoring signals such as Take-Two’s upcoming earnings updates and any new marketing drops, which could influence whether events like major album releases, AI milestones, or political shifts occur first. The market reflects broad uncertainty around these timelines, given Rockstar’s track record of last-minute refinements and the game’s outsized cultural footprint.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York retrial, declared May 15 after jurors deadlocked on the remaining rape charge, combined with his ongoing appeal of the 2022 California conviction and 16-year sentence, has driven trader consensus toward no additional prison time. At 74 and reporting ongoing health issues while held at Rikers, Weinstein faces legal uncertainty that markets interpret as likely to yield concurrent sentencing, plea resolutions, or successful appeals rather than stacked terms exceeding current incarceration. Historical patterns of overturned #MeToo-era convictions and the high bar for retrials reinforce this implied probability, though any California appeal denial or new charges could still shift momentum before final resolution.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final pits the Carolina Hurricanes against the Vegas Golden Knights in a best-of-seven series tied at one game apiece following a 5-4 Vegas win in Game 1 and a 4-3 overtime Carolina victory in Game 2. Carolina’s league-best 113 regular-season points, Eastern Conference title, and dominant playoff path—including sweeps of the Senators and Flyers plus a five-game Eastern Final win over Montreal—position the Hurricanes as the trader favorite. Vegas advanced by defeating the Avalanche in the Western Final after strong Pacific Division play. The narrow implied probabilities reflect Carolina’s superior record and recent form offset by Vegas’s experience in high-stakes series and the even series score.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks have surged to overwhelming 2026 NBA Championship favorite status after seizing a 2-0 lead in the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs, including road victories of 105-95 and 105-104 that featured strong contributions from Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, Mikal Bridges, and Josh Hart. The Spurs entered as the top Western Conference team with a 62-20 record and Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way play, yet their early series deficits have shifted trader consensus sharply toward New York completing the upset in the remaining games at home. This momentum from the Knicks' playoff run and the Spurs' home losses has driven the current implied probabilities, with limited time remaining for San Antonio to mount a comeback before potential clinchers.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks have surged to overwhelming 2026 NBA Championship favorite status after seizing a 2-0 lead in the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs, including road victories of 105-95 and 105-104 that featured strong contributions from Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, Mikal Bridges, and Josh Hart. The Spurs entered as the top Western Conference team with a 62-20 record and Victor Wembanyama's elite two-way play, yet their early series deficits have shifted trader consensus sharply toward New York completing the upset in the remaining games at home. This momentum from the Knicks' playoff run and the Spurs' home losses has driven the current implied probabilities, with limited time remaining for San Antonio to mount a comeback before potential clinchers.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share the narrowest edge in trader consensus for the 2026 World Cup title at 16.2% implied probability each, reflecting their superior squad depth, recent form, and attacking talent ahead of the expanded 48-team tournament opening June 11. Spain benefits from an unbeaten run in recent matches and the emergence of young stars like Lamine Yamal, while France draws on consistent major-tournament experience and defensive organization. England sits at 11.3% thanks to its balanced roster and home-continent familiarity, with Argentina at 8.8% leveraging defending-champion pedigree. The tight clustering among these sides, plus Portugal and Brazil, stems from comparable strengths in midfield control, set-piece execution, and qualification paths, leaving realistic paths for several contenders to advance deep into the knockout stages.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's removal of presidential term limits in 2018 and his re-election to a third term as CCP general secretary at the 2020 Party Congress established the structural basis for extended rule. The 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027 is widely viewed as the venue for a fourth term, with the intervening 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) framed in official addresses as carrying his personal imprint. Recent signals, including his 2026 New Year message and ongoing personnel placements, reinforce party unity around his leadership amid economic and external pressures. Trader pricing at 92.8% for continuation reflects the absence of verifiable challenges to his authority and the historical pattern of incumbents prevailing at party congresses.

Contenders including Jon Ossoff, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg differentiate through Senate or gubernatorial performance in competitive states and alignment with key voter coalitions. With no formal declarations as of mid-2026 and the midterms approaching, support consolidation would likely depend on polling shifts, donor momentum, and primary-state outreach ahead of Iowa.
